hero.titulo: Data Scientist | Python | Machine Learning | SQL | Power BI | AWS
hero.subtitulo: Bridging strategic business vision with technical precision in Data Science and Analytics.

sobre.paragrafo1: I am a Data Scientist who combines a strategic business perspective with technical precision in technology. I hold a degree in Business Administration, a postgraduate degree in IT Governance, and I am currently pursuing a degree in Systems Analysis and Development.
sobre.paragrafo2: With complementary training in Data Science, completed in 2024, I leverage my multidisciplinary experience across the banking, healthcare, and technology sectors to transform complex data into analytical solutions that generate real value.
sobre.paragrafo3: My expertise includes Python, SQL, NoSQL with MongoDB, Power BI, Tableau, Excel, exploratory data analysis, Machine Learning model development, and the creation of strategic dashboards to support data-driven decision-making.

experiencia.desc1_item1: Data collection and extraction in SQL databases across AWS, Azure, and Oracle environments.
experiencia.desc1_item2: Data treatment, cleaning, and exploratory analysis with Python, Pandas, and Power Query.
experiencia.desc1_item3: Development of interactive dashboards and reports in Power BI, Metabase, Grafana, and Jupyter Notebook.
experiencia.desc1_item4: Generation and presentation of strategic insights to support data-driven decision making.
experiencia.desc1_item5: Participation in implementation projects and task automation using PyAutoGUI.

experiencia.desc2_item2: Analysis of graphic records, contracts, and client dossiers.
experiencia.desc2_item3: Issuance, verification, and correction of discharge letters.
experiencia.desc2_item4: Support for renegotiation processes and documentary control.

projeto1.resumo: Machine Learning model developed to classify patients at risk of diabetes based on clinical, demographic and lifestyle data. The project involved exploratory analysis, class balancing, algorithm comparison, LightGBM training and Streamlit deployment.
projeto1.tecnologias: Python, Pandas, Scikit-learn, PyCaret, LightGBM, Streamlit
projeto1.descricao:
Machine Learning project developed to classify patients as diabetic or not based on health indicators, demographic data and lifestyle habits. The solution uses the Diabetes Health Indicators Dataset, with over 253 thousand records, and covers the entire Data Science project workflow: exploratory analysis, variable treatment, feature selection, class balancing, model training, performance evaluation and Streamlit deployment.

The final model was built with LightGBM after comparison with different classification algorithms. The solution showed consistent performance across training, testing and validation sets, reaching an AUC over 0.97 and approximately 92% accuracy in the validation stage. The project demonstrates the practical application of Machine Learning in healthcare, focusing on identifying patterns associated with diabetes risk and supporting data-driven decision making.
projeto1.insight1: Analysis of a dataset with over 253 thousand health and lifestyle records
projeto1.insight2: Data treatment, normalization and balancing with SMOTE
projeto1.insight3: Model selection and comparison using PyCaret
projeto1.insight5: Evaluation with accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, confusion matrix and ROC curve
projeto1.insight6: Model deployment in a Streamlit app for interactive use
projeto1.resultados:
The LightGBM model showed consistent performance in the training, test and validation sets. In the automated comparison stage with PyCaret, LightGBM achieved 91.61% accuracy, 0.9700 AUC, 0.9599 precision and 0.9119 F1-score.

After manual tuning and validation, the model maintained good generalization capacity, reaching an AUC of 0.9753 in training, 0.9716 in testing and 0.9701 in validation, with consistent precision, recall and F1-score metrics.

projeto2.resumo: Exploratory credit data analysis aimed at identifying default patterns. The project used Python, Pandas, Seaborn and Matplotlib to process financial data, compare profiles of compliant and defaulting customers, and generate strategic insights for credit decision making.
projeto2.tecnologias: Python, Pandas, Seaborn, Matplotlib, EDA, Credit Risk
projeto2.descricao:
This project performs an exploratory credit data analysis aimed at identifying patterns associated with customer default in a financial institution. The analysis uses a dataset with 10,127 customers and 16 variables, including age, education, salary range, card type, credit limit, number of products, transaction value, and number of transactions in the last 12 months.

The work involved reading and understanding the dataset, analyzing categorical and numerical data, handling missing values, converting monetary variables to numerical format, and creating comparative visualizations between compliant and defaulting customers. The goal was to support more informed decisions on credit risk, loss prevention, and improvement in financial product offerings.
projeto2.insight1: Analysis of a dataset with 10,127 customers and 16 financial and behavioral variables
projeto2.insight2: Identification of an initial default rate of 16.07% in the analyzed dataset
projeto2.insight3: Treatment of missing data in education, marital status and annual salary
projeto2.insight4: Conversion of monetary variables from text to numerical format
projeto2.insight5: Visual comparison between compliant and defaulting customers
projeto2.insight6: Generation of insights regarding product quantity, transactions, credit limit and spending behavior
projeto2.resultados:
The analysis showed that defaulting customers exhibited specific behavioral patterns. Generally, they had between 2 and 3 contracted products, while compliant customers tended to have between 3 and 6 products. It was also observed that defaulting customers concentrated their transactions at lower values, averaging around R$ 2,500.00 in the last 12 months and not exceeding values above R$ 10,000.00 in the analyzed period.

Another relevant point was the relationship between transaction value and quantity. Customers with higher financial volume and transaction count showed a lower tendency to default, whereas customers with lower value and frequency transactions appeared more associated with the defaulting group. Additionally, credit limits above R$ 20,000.00 appeared predominantly among compliant customers.

projeto3.resumo: Computer vision project focused on beverage waste classification using YOLOv5. The model was trained to detect and differentiate recyclable objects of aluminum and glass classes, from images labeled in YOLO format. The solution demonstrates a practical application of Machine Learning to support selective collection processes, reduce waste and increase recycling efficiency.
projeto3.tecnologias: Python, YOLOv5, PyTorch, OpenCV, LabelImg, Machine Learning
projeto3.descricao: Computer vision project developed at BairesDev - Machine Learning Practitioner Bootcamp, focusing on recyclable beverage waste detection. The YOLOv5 model was trained to recognize aluminum cans and glass bottles, using bounding box labeled images and validation with separate data. The proposal demonstrates how Machine Learning techniques can support selective collection, recycling and sustainability processes.
projeto3.insight1: Creation and organization of a dataset with aluminum and glass classes
projeto3.insight2: Image labeling in YOLO format using LabelImg
projeto3.insight3: YOLOv5 network training with PyTorch for object detection
projeto3.insight4: Model validation on real images with bounding boxes
projeto3.insight5: Practical application focused on recycling and waste separation
